label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know why social networking sites are harmful according to the given text. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the content of the article. The main point is that while sites like Facebook and Twitter help people connect, they also lead to increased isolation. The article mentions that using these sites reduces face-to-face interactions, which in turn can cause biological changes and increase health risks like cancer, strokes, etc. Dr. Sigman is cited as saying that the problem is more isolation due to less direct communication. Looking at the options: A) They fail to bring users the pleasure they want. The article actually says these sites provide pleasure, so this isn't correct. B) They don't bring users enough convenience. The article doesn't focus on convenience, so this is likely wrong. C) More isolation is caused by less direct communication. This matches the article's main argument about decreased face-to-face time leading to isolation and health
